description: LTX-2 19B text-to-video GENERATION with the CAMERA-CONTROL LoRAs as the headline. The 19B base is a SEPARATE model family from LTX-2.3 22B (so it has its own LoRA catalog, ltx2-19b.loras.json). This graph ships two camera-LoRA slots and a built-in two-stage path (base render + 2x latent upscale) plus an optional Gemma prompt-enhancer. Stack a camera move (dolly in/out/left/right, jib up/down, static) from the catalog with --lora. Graph: Lightricks/ComfyUI-LTXVideo example 'LTX-2_T2V_Distilled_wLora' (example_workflows/2.0).

prompt_guide.tips: Write ONE flowing paragraph like a cinematographer's shot list, in order: open with the action; then movement & gestures; appearance; the setting; camera angle/move; lighting & mood; any changes or sudden events. Be literal and specific (not abstract). Keep it under ~200 words; longer prompts fill longer clips.

about.what: Creates a brand-new video from a written description on the LTX-2 19B model, with controllable CAMERA MOVES you add from the LoRA list (dolly in/out, track left/right, crane up/down, or locked-off static). Includes a built-in 2x high-detail pass.
about.inputs: A written description of the scene and action. Add one (or more) camera-move LoRAs with the LoRA picker.
about.key_knobs: Prompt — describe the scene AND the DESTINATION of the camera move (where it ends up / what it reveals). Camera LoRA — which move. Frames — clip length (8*n+1).

_TEMPLATE: READY-TO-RUN. All weights are pinned to real, ungated HF URLs: the 19B distilled checkpoint + the LTX-2 spatial upscaler x2 (Lightricks/LTX-2) and the Gemma-3-12B text encoder FOLDER (Lightricks/gemma-3-12b-it-qat-q4_0-unquantized — 16 files, listed below because the 19B graph's LTXVGemmaCLIPModelLoader reads a HF model directory, not a single file). Run --convert-only FIRST.
_CAMERA: The camera moves live in the SEPARATE 19B catalog (loras/ltx2-19b.loras.json) because they are 19B-only (a different base than the 2.3-22B presets). Use them with --lora: e.g. `--lora dolly-in` or `--lora jib-up:0.8`. They have NO trigger word — instead DESCRIBE THE DESTINATION of the move in the prompt. `--list-loras --catalog ltx2-19b` shows the menu. They stack onto the checkpoint (lora_chain anchor 5228), which feeds every model path into the sampler subgraph (the graph's own two bypassed camera slots are left bypassed; the stack supersedes them).
_SUBGRAPH: The 2.0 'wLora' graph is SUBGRAPHED (one subgraph holds the two-stage sampler + upscaler). comfy_run.py's converter flattens subgraphs at convert time (see WORKFLOW_INGEST.md G), so it converts without a hand-written derive. Because the width/height (EmptyLTXVLatentVideo) live INSIDE the subgraph and get renumbered on flatten, resolution is left at the graph default and not exposed as a knob here; prompt + length + camera are top-level and stable.
_PROMPT_ENHANCE: The graph includes an LTXVGemmaEnhancePrompt node (the official prompt enhancer) feeding the text encode. It runs on the loaded Gemma encoder, no API key.
_HOWITRUNS: No-build path: public yanwk image, comfy_run.py clones node_packs, downloads the weights (HEAVY 19B cold start: ~38GB checkpoint + ~24GB Gemma folder + upscaler), flattens + converts the subgraph UI graph to API, splices the --lora camera stack onto anchor 5228, and renders the two-stage result. rtxpro6000 recommended; use --idle-hold to keep a warm node.

ui.title: LTX-2 19B Generate — text-to-video with camera-control LoRAs

prompt.default: A lone astronaut stands at the rim of a vast canyon on a red planet, two moons low on the horizon, dust drifting in a thin wind. Cinematic, epic scale, golden rim light.

ui.label: Prompt
ui.tooltip: Describe the scene AND, when using a camera-move LoRA, the DESTINATION of the move (where the camera ends up / what it reveals) — that's how Lightricks' camera LoRAs are steered (no trigger word). One flowing present-tense paragraph. (src: LTX-2 camera-control model cards + LTX prompt guide)

ui.label: Frames (length)
ui.tooltip: Frame count; LTX REQUIRES 8*n+1 (..., 121, 161). 121 ~= 4s. (src: LTX-2 docs)
